channel.title: Code Defence Intelligence Brief — Thu, 26 Mar 2026
channel.description: Daily cybersecurity intelligence for Thu, 26 Mar 2026.

item.title: APT28 weaponizes LLMs with PROMPTSTEAL malware for automated theft. 🤖
item.description:
State-sponsored attackers are now using AI to automate the generation of malicious system commands in real-time. 🤖
Threat Alert · PROMPTSTEAL malware attributed to APT28 ❨FROZENLAKE❩ identifies and exfiltrates documents.
In a structural shift for cyberespionage‚ researchers have identified PROMPTSTEAL‚ a malware family that actively queries Large Language Models during live operations. Attributed to the Russian government-backed actor APT28‚ the malware uses LLMs to generate one-line Windows commands tailored to the victim environment to facilitate silent document theft.
This marks the first confirmed observation of a state-sponsored actor integrating AI queries directly into a malware kill-chain. By leveraging AI to adapt to the specific file structures of a target system‚ the attackers can bypass traditional static detection rules and accelerate the speed of data exfiltration.
The uncomfortable truth: Your defense-in-depth model is being outpaced by an adversary that now uses the same AI productivity tools as your developers to automate the destruction of your perimeter.
→ Audit your network for anomalous outbound API traffic to known AI provider endpoints originating from non-authorized service accounts. → Implement strict application control to prevent the execution of unsigned or unauthorized scripts generated on-the-fly. → Monitor for unusual document access patterns on executive workstations and sensitive file shares.
#Cybersecurity #AISecurity #APT28 #Infosec #SecurityLeadership #CodeDefence

item.title: Interlock ransomware weaponizes Cisco zero-day for mass intrusion. 🛑
item.description:
A critical firewall management flaw was exploited for weeks before defenders were even aware it existed. 🛑
CVE-2026-20131 · Severity 10.0 · Interlock Ransomware targeting Cisco Secure FMC.
The Interlock ransomware group has been confirmed as the primary actor exploiting a maximum-severity flaw in @[Cisco](urn:li:organization:1063) Secure Firewall Management Center ❨FMC❩. Investigations reveal the group had access to this zero-day and was using it to establish persistent root backdoors several weeks before the public disclosure and patch release.
Once initial access is achieved via the management interface‚ the attackers deploy memory-resident backdoors and BASH-based relay networks to hide their true location. This allows them to pivot laterally into the Windows environment and enumerate data before deploying the final ransomware payload.
The uncomfortable truth: Edge devices like firewalls and VPNs are no longer just barriers; they are the high-value pivot points that ransomware groups now prioritize for unauthenticated entry.
→ Immediately upgrade Cisco Secure FMC to the latest fixed release to close CVE-2026-20131. → Conduct a retrospective audit of your management logs for any unauthorized administrative account creation dating back to January 2026. → Ensure that all management interfaces are strictly isolated from the public internet using a secure VPN or OOB network.
#Cybersecurity #Ransomware #Cisco #EdgeSecurity #SOC #CodeDefence

item.title: TeamPCP backdoors LiteLLM AI package in ongoing supply-chain campaign. 🐛
item.description:
The supply-chain campaign that hit Trivy and Checkmarx has moved into the AI development ecosystem. 🐛
Supply Chain Alert · TeamPCP poisoning LiteLLM PyPI package versions 1.82.7 and 1.82.8.
The threat group TeamPCP has successfully compromised the LiteLLM Python package on PyPI. By leveraging credentials stolen during earlier attacks on @[GitHub](urn:li:organization:19041) Actions‚ the group injected a persistent credential stealer into versions 1.82.7 and 1.82.8 of the library.
The attack utilizes a .pth file hijack‚ which ensures the malicious payload executes every time any Python script is run in the environment‚ regardless of whether LiteLLM is explicitly imported. This technique allows for silent‚ widespread persistence across AI development workstations and cloud clusters.
The uncomfortable truth: Your speed-to-market with AI is creating unvetted dependency chains that are now the primary targets for industrialized credential harvesting.
→ Immediately uninstall LiteLLM versions 1.82.7 and 1.82.8 and revert to a verified release. → Rotate all AWS/Azure/GCP tokens and SSH keys that were present on any system where the malicious package was installed. → Enforce pin-to-hash policies for all third-party Python dependencies in your build pipelines.
#Cybersecurity #SupplyChain #DevSecOps #AISecurity #AppSec #CodeDefence

item.title: CISA orders federal agencies to patch critical Craft CMS and Apple flaws. 🛡️
item.description:
The US cyber security agency has added five critical vulnerabilities to the KEV catalog following active exploitation. 🛡️
KEV Update · Craft CMS and Apple WebKit flaws targeted in live attacks.
The @[CISA](urn:li:organization:13010360) has added five security flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog‚ including a maximum-severity code injection in Craft CMS ❨CVE-2025-32432❩ and multiple @[Apple](urn:li:organization:162479) WebKit issues. These vulnerabilities are being leveraged in targeted cyberespionage campaigns to gain root control over mobile devices and web servers.
The Apple-related flaws are core components of the DarkSword exploit kit‚ which allows for the silent exfiltration of sensitive data—including passwords and health records—when a user visits a malicious website. Federal agencies are mandated to remediate these flaws by April 3‚ 2026.
The uncomfortable truth: Your public-facing content management systems and mobile browser engines are the two most volatile entry points in your modern enterprise architecture.
→ Update all @[Apple](urn:li:organization:162479) devices to the latest iOS 26.x and macOS 16.x releases today to neutralize WebKit exploits. → Patch Craft CMS and Laravel Livewire instances to their latest security releases immediately. → Enable Lockdown Mode for high-value targets to reduce the attack surface for complex memory corruption flaws.
#Cybersecurity #AppSec #MobileSecurity #Apple #CISA #CodeDefence
